mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS snapshot value was 442032.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in more than 60% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE snapshot value was 5.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing below 0.25% signals binding monetary accommodation that lowers discount rates and supports equity duration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS snapshot value was 0.13 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the average 60-day S&P 500 return after FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% is not positive or is below the unconditional 60-day average return.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving utilization and goods-sector earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO snapshot value was 105.7065 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO growth has a non-positive rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth accompanied by a falling unemployment rate identifies broad labor-market healing that raises income expectations without signaling immediate stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS snapshot value was 141842.0 and UNRATE was 5.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods with only one of the two labor improvements.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains below 1% signals a negative real-rate inflation shock that raises future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL snapshot value was 237.031 as of t-2mo and FEDFUNDS was 0.13 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-pressure episodes have positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns and no lower return distribution than non-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above 1000 thousand annualized units signals improving construction activity and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST snapshot value was 1036.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings above 1000 are followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals broad demand impairment and lower aggregate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after the GDP signal is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 snapshot value was 16287.7 as of t-6mo
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth observations are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered instances.
